back_translation: And Elihu went on to say:
'Do you think this is just? You say, 'I am more righteous than God.'
For you ask, 'What do I gain, and what do I profit more than if I had sinned?'
I will answer you, and your friends with you.
Look at the heavens and consider; gaze at the clouds high above you.
If you sin, what do you do against Him? If you multiply your transgressions, what do you do to Him?
If you are righteous, what do you give Him, or what does He receive from your hand?
Your wickedness affects only a man like yourself, and your righteousness only a son of man.
People cry out under heavy oppression; they plead for relief from the power of the mighty.
But no one asks, 'Where is God my Maker, who gives songs in the night,
who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth and makes us wiser than the birds of the air?'
There they cry out, but He does not answer, because of the pride of evil men.
Surely God does not heed an empty plea, and the Almighty does not pay attention to it.
How much less, then, when you say you do not see Him, though your case is before Him and you must wait for Him,
and further, when you say He has not yet punished in His anger or paid much attention to folly.
So Job opens his mouth in vain and multiplies words without knowledge.'
